title: 10 best herbal teas for swollen gums
intro_desc: Herbal teas for swollen gums are natural remedies made from herbs and plants that help reduce inflammation and soothe discomfort in the gums. These teas typically contain anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help fight off infection and promote healthy gums. Examples of herbal teas that help with swollen gums include chamomile, clove, sage, and calendula. Chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and soothe pain, while clove tea contains eugenol, a natural anesthetic that numbs the gums and reduces inflammation. Sage tea has antibacterial and astringent properties that help reduce inflammation and prevent infection, and calendula tea has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that promote healing. Herbal teas for swollen gums can significantly improve the lives of those suffering from gum disease, as they provide a natural and effective way to manage symptoms and promote oral health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera teas helps with swollen gums because its anti-inflammatory properties can reduce redness, swelling, and pain. The tea's natural astringent properties also help to constrict blood vessels, which reduces bleeding and promotes healing. Additionally, the soothing compounds in aloe vera tea calm irritated gum tissue, providing relief from discomfort and promoting a healthy oral environment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce swelling and alleviate pain by reducing inflammation in the gums.

Antimicrobial: The antibacterial properties of aloe vera tea, including its ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, may help combat infections that can cause gum swelling and prevent further infection.
Antioxidant: Aloe vera's high antioxidant content helps neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue and contribute to swelling, promoting overall oral health and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Aloe roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and ease pain in gums.
Bark: The bark of the aloe vera plant has been traditionally used to treat gum inflammation, providing relief from discomfort and pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves from a mature plant or use dried aloe vera powder measuring out 10-15 grams per serving.
Wash the fresh leaves thoroughly with warm water and remove any spines or thorns using a vegetable peeler.
Cut the peeled leaves into small pieces and soak them in boiling water for at least 30 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean pot discarding the solids after 20-25 minutes of steeping time.
Add honey to taste and simmer the tea for an additional 5-10 minutes before serving warm or at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with swollen gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort. The tea's flavonoids and carotenoids work together to calm irritated gum tissues, while also combating bacterial infections that can exacerbate the condition. Additionally, the tea's soothing properties help to relax tense jaw muscles and promote a sense of calm, making it an effective natural remedy for swollen gums.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Calendula, the active compound found in pot marigold tea, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in pot marigold tea can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to gum inflammation and damage, promoting overall oral health.
Wound-healing properties: Pot marigold tea has been shown to stimulate collagen synthesis and improve wound healing, which can help reduce the severity of swollen gums and promote faster recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Potent anti-inflammatory properties make roots effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation.
Bark: The astringent and antibacterial compounds in the bark help to reduce gum bleeding, soothe inflammation, and prevent further infection.
remedy_recipe: Gather one cup of dried calendula officinalis flowers or four teaspoons of fresh flowers.
Heat one quart of water in a large saucepan over medium heat for seven minutes.
Add the dried or fresh calendula flowers to the hot water and let steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup or teapot.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving it warm or chilled with honey to taste.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Teaberry teas helps with swollen gums because its natural ingredients, such as tea tree oil and chamomile, possess anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and alleviate pain. The antioxidant-rich compounds in teaberry teas also help to combat bacteria and plaque buildup, promoting healthy gum tissue and preventing further irritation. Additionally, the soothing effects of teaberry teas calm inflammation and discomfort, providing relief for sensitive gums and mouth sores.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Teaberry tea contains antibacterial compounds such as methyl salicylate, which helps to combat bacterial infections in the mouth and reduce inflammation in swollen gums.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in teaberry tea have anti-inflammatory effects, reducing swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation and promoting healing of affected areas.
Antioxidant properties: Teaberry tea's antioxidant compounds help to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to gum disease and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with swollen gums.
Bark: Has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe irritated gum tissue and reduce swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems in early summer morning for optimal flavor.
Rinse the herb mixture with filtered water to remove any impurities or debris from the environment.
Combine one teaspoon of loose-leaf teaberry with 8 ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass teapot.
Steep the herbal mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the bioactive compounds and flavors to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids and serving immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with swollen gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and alleviate pain. The tea contains compounds like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids that inhibit the production of enzymes responsible for breaking down connective tissue, thereby reducing inflammation and soothing irritated gum tissue. Additionally, herbal licorice tea has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections that can cause gum inflammation, making it a natural remedy to promote healthy gums and alleviate discomfort.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly glycyrrhizin and isoliquiritigenin, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in inflamed gums.
Saponins: Saponins, a type of triterpenoid glycoside, have been shown to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help combat gum infections and reduce inflammation.
Phytosterols: Phytosterols, such as beta-sitosterol, have been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums, while also improving oral health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in gums.
Bark: The bark of the licorice plant contains a flavonoid called licoricidin, which has antibacterial properties that help combat gum infections and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried licorice root for every eight ounces of water.
Heat the measured water in a saucepan over medium heat for five minutes.
Add the dried licorice root to the boiling water and remove from heat immediately.
Steep the mixture for seven to ten minutes then strain it into a cup using a tea strainer.
Serve the tea hot or warm and enjoy within 24 hours of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Witch hazel teas helps with swollen gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and pain. The astringent compounds in witch hazel help constrict blood vessels, thereby reducing bleeding and inflammation. Additionally, the tea's antibacterial qualities combat bacteria that contribute to gum infections. Regular consumption of witch hazel tea may also improve oral hygiene by soothing irritated gums and promoting healthy tissue growth, leading to reduced swelling and improved overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Tannins: Witch hazel contains high levels of tannins, which have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and fight off bacterial infections in the gums.

Alkaloids: The alkaloid content in witch hazel tea, particularly berberine and hydrastine, has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce gum inflammation and alleviate pain associated with swollen gums.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in witch hazel tea, such as quercetin, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and prevent further damage to the gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in tannins, which help reduce inflammation and constrict blood vessels to alleviate swelling.
Bark: Containing salicin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound similar to aspirin, helping to relieve gum pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality witch hazel leaves and twigs from wild or cultivated sources.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm, dark place for at least two weeks.
Combine one tablespoon of dried witch hazel with eight ounces of boiling water and steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a heat-proof cup or teapot.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving and enjoy within three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St john's wort teas helps with swollen gums because it possesses anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. The tea's flavonoids and phenolic acids work to combat inflammation-causing free radicals, soothing the affected area and promoting healthy gum tissue growth. Additionally, St john's wort has natural antibacterial properties that aid in preventing infections, which can contribute to swollen gums.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: St. John's Wort tea contains antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to swollen gums by reducing the severity of gum disease.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in St. John's Wort tea have anti-inflammatory effects that may help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum disease, making it easier to manage symptoms like swollen gums.

Antimicrobial properties: St. John's Wort tea has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against certain bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can cause gum infections and inflammation, which can contribute to swollen gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of St. John's Wort contain active compounds that help reduce inflammation and swelling, making them effective in treating gum inflammation.
Bark: The bark of St. John's Wort contains tannins, which have astringent properties that can help constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding from swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried st john's wort leaves and flowers at dawn to ensure optimal potency.
Rinse the st john's wort mixture with cold filtered water to remove impurities and debris.
Combine one teaspoon of dried st john's wort with one cup of boiling water for a 5-7 minute infusion time.
Strain the tea using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve to separate the liquid from the solids.
Store the prepared st john's wort tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with swollen gums because its antibacterial properties can help combat the underlying infections that cause gum inflammation. The tea's antiseptic qualities can also reduce redness, swelling, and pain in the affected area, promoting a sense of calm and relief. Additionally, the calming effects of lavender may help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for gum sensitivity and swelling.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lavender tea's antimicrobial properties help to combat bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can cause gum infections, reducing inflammation and swelling in the process.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lavender tea, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, help to reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with swollen gums.
Antioxidant properties: Lavender tea's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue, promoting overall oral health and helping to alleviate symptoms of swollen gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing swelling and alleviating pain in swollen gums.
Leaves: Containing flavonoids and terpenes that help reduce inflammation, ease gum pain, and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried english lavender flowers at dawn for optimal potency.
Measure out two teaspoons of dried english lavender flowers or one teaspoon of fresh flowers for every eight ounces water.
Heat filtered water in a saucepan to a gentle simmer and steep the lavender flowers for five to seven minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup and discard the solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving, garnishing with a slice of lemon if desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile teas helps with swollen gums because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and alleviate pain. The tea's flavonoids and terpenoids work together to inhibit the production of enzymes that cause inflammation, soothing inflamed tissue and promoting a healthy gum environment. Additionally, German chamomile has antiseptic properties, helping to combat bacteria that can exacerbate gum issues, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving swollen gums.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Chamomile tea's antibacterial compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help combat bacteria that can cause gum inflammation and swelling.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in chamomile tea, including quercetin and kaempferol, reduce inflammation in the gums, which can alleviate swelling and discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: Chamomile tea's antioxidant compounds help neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue, leading to inflammation and swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation.
Flowers: Rich in apigenin, an flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe inflamed gum tissue.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Heat fresh filtered water to a boil and pour it over the measured chamomile flowers in a tea infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes or until the desired strength is reached according to personal taste.
Strain the tea into a cup using a strainer or tea infuser and discard the solids.
Serve the tea hot or allow it to cool to room temperature for a soothing and calming beverage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with swollen gums because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's flavonoids, such as rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, help to reduce swelling and ease pain associated with gum inflammation. Additionally, lemon balm has antimicrobial properties that combat bacterial infections, which can contribute to swollen gums. By drinking herbal lemon balm teas, individuals may experience relief from discomfort and promote a healthy mouth environment.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lemon balm tea's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial infections that can cause gum swelling by inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria and promoting a healthy oral environment.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon balm tea, such as rosmarinic acid, help reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums, providing relief from pain and discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm tea's antioxidant properties help protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to gum inflammation and disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are valued for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums.
Leaves: The leaves contain compounds that have antibacterial effects, helping to combat gum infections and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried melissa officinalis leaves, measuring one teaspoon per eight ounces of water for brewing.
Heat fresh filtered water in a saucepan to boiling point and remove from heat source immediately.
Add the measured dried melissa officinalis leaves to the hot water and let steep for seven to ten minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container, discarding solids.
Chill the herbal lemon balm tea in the refrigerator for at least three hours before serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oregano teas helps with swollen gums because of its antibacterial properties, which combat the underlying infections that cause gum inflammation. The tea's active compounds, such as carvacrol and thymol, work to reduce bacterial growth and alleviate swelling by fighting off harmful pathogens that can cause periodontal disease. Additionally, oregano's anti-inflammatory properties help soothe irritated gums, promoting a healthy balance of oral flora and reducing pain and discomfort associated with swollen gums.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial Properties: Oregano tea's antimicrobial properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms in the mouth, which can contribute to swollen gums.
Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in oregano tea, such as rosmarinic acid and thymol, help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums.
Antioxidant Activity: Oregano tea's antioxidant properties help to protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to gum inflammation and swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, oregano roots help reduce inflammation and swelling in gums.
Bark: Containing tannins and flavonoids, oregano bark tea has astringent properties that can help alleviate gum pain and bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Gather one tablespoon of dried oregano vulgare leaves and 250 milliliters of boiling water.
Steep the dried oregano leaves in the boiling water for five to seven minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a teapot or cup.
Add one tablespoon of honey or sugar to sweeten the tea, if desired, and stir well.
Serve the herbal oregano tea warm, allowing it to cool for at least two minutes before consumption.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat swollen gums with herbal teas is to use cold chamomile or sage tea bags. Steep the tea bags in hot water for a few minutes, then let them cool down. Place the cooled tea bags directly on the affected area for about 10-15 minutes. Both chamomile and sage have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help reduce swelling and soothe irritation. Repeat this process several times a day for best results. Additionally, maintain good oral hygiene by brushing and flossing regularly.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to swollen gums that are treated with herbal teas include gingivitis and periodontal disease. Herbal teas like chamomile and clove have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce gum inflammation and pain. Green tea is rich in antioxidants that promote healthy gums and prevent gum diseases. Licorice root tea inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth, reducing the risk of gum diseases. Myrrh tea has antiseptic and astringent properties that soothe inflamed gums and promote healing. Regular consumption of these herbal teas can alleviate symptoms of gum ailments and improve oral health.
